title: jacquemontia bracteosa
intro: Jacquemontia bracteosa, also known as the coastal mercury vine, is a climbing plant native to tropical and subtropical regions. This plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Jacquemontia bracteosa include treating fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as a natural remedy for wounds and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, Jacquemontia bracteosa is often combined with other medicinal plants to create teas, infusions, and topical ointments that take advantage of its medicinal properties.
